title: Fluorescent Immunolabelling for endogenous ChAT in mice striatum

steps: Wash: 3 x 00:10:00 gently on a shaker, in TBST at Room temperature .
Blocking step: Incubate the slides with blocking solution on the shaker for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Primary antibody: incubate the slides with the blocking solution/Antibody on a shaker at 4 °C for 24:00:00 48:00:00 .
Washes: 3 x 00:10:00 on a shaker, in TBST.
incubate the slides with Secondary antibody solution 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Incubation the slides 00:10:00 with the DAPI solution.
Washes: 2 times, for 00:10:00 each in TBST.
Coverslip: mounted with prolong anti-fade reagent (Invitrogen).
Select floating sections of mouse striatum (30um thick).
During the washes, prepare blocking solution: 10 % donkey serum (DS) in TBST.
Before the end of the blocking step, prepare the solution with the primary antibody ChAT blocking solution (TBST+10% DS) + Goat Anti-Chat Ab  dilution 1:200.
During the washes, prepare the secondary Antibody solution blocking solution (TBST+10% DS) + anti donkey anti goat Alexa Fluor 1:1000.
Optional: DAPI staining During the washes, prepare the DAPI solution DAPI (1 mg/ml). Dilution 1:10.000 in TBST.
creator: Jacquelyn Haytayan
